claimText: The Asian Giant Softshell Turtle is classified as Critically Endangered on the IUCN Red List and is protected under Schedule I of the Wildlife Protection Act, 1972.
evidenceExcerpt: IUCN Status is Critically Endangered (CR) - Listed in WPA, 1972 Schedule I and CITES Appendix II
examUse: Statement-based question on conservation status of species in news.
distractorHooks: Schedule II or III of WPA

claimText: The Asian Giant Softshell Turtle inhabits large lowland rivers, estuaries, and coastal mudflats across South and South-east Asia.
evidenceExcerpt: Found in large lowland rivers, estuaries, and coastal mudflats in South and South-east Asia
examUse: Statement-based question on habitat and geographical distribution.
distractorHooks: Found only in high-altitude freshwater lakes

claimText: In Kerala, the Asian Giant Softshell Turtle is locally known as Bhimanama.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching species to their local names and regions.
distractorHooks: Bhimanama is a local name in Odisha or West Bengal
Bhimanama refers to a new fish species

claimText: The Asian Giant Softshell Turtle is an ambush predator characterized by a flattened head and aggressive, high-speed striking behavior.
evidenceExcerpt: Ambush predators with aggressive behavior and a lightning speed strike
examUse: Statement-based question on species characteristics and feeding behavior.
